name: The Sun
tagline: The Heart of the Solar System
description: The Sun is a nearly perfect sphere of hot plasma, heated to incandescence by nuclear fusion reactions in its core. It radiates energy mainly as light, ultraviolet, and infrared radiation, providing the primary energy source for life on Earth.

hotspots.name: Nuclear Core
hotspots.description: At the core, gravitational forces compress hydrogen atoms until they fuse into helium, releasing enormous energy.

hotspots.name: Solar Corona
hotspots.description: The outermost atmosphere of the Sun, reaching temperatures of millions of degrees, venting solar wind into space.

name: Mercury
description: Mercury is the smallest and closest planet to the Sun. With no atmosphere to retain heat, it experiences the most extreme temperature swings in the Solar System, ranging from blistering heat during the day to freezing cold at night.

hotspots.name: Caloris Basin
hotspots.description: One of the largest impact basins in the Solar System, created by a massive asteroid collision early in Mercury's history.

name: Venus
description: Venus is our closest planetary neighbor, similar in structure and size to Earth but vastly different. Its thick, toxic atmosphere traps heat in a runaway greenhouse effect, making it the hottest planet in our solar system.

hotspots.name: Maat Mons
hotspots.description: The highest volcano on Venus, rising 8 kilometers above the mean planetary radius. It may still be volcanically active.

name: Earth
description: Earth is our home planet, the third planet from the Sun, and the only place we know of so far that's inhabited by living things. Its atmosphere, rich in oxygen and moisture, moderates global temperatures to allow liquid water oceans.

hotspots.name: Mariana Trench
hotspots.description: The deepest known point in Earth's oceans, stretching down nearly 11 kilometers in the western Pacific.

hotspots.name: The Himalayas
hotspots.description: Earth's highest mountain range, formed by the collision of tectonic plates and containing Mount Everest.

name: Mars
description: Mars is a dusty, cold, desert world with a very thin atmosphere. There is strong evidence that Mars was—billions of years ago—wetter and warmer, with a thicker atmosphere, making it a primary target for astrobiology.

hotspots.name: Olympus Mons
hotspots.description: The largest volcano in the Solar System, three times the height of Mount Everest and broad enough to cover the state of Arizona.

hotspots.name: Valles Marineris
hotspots.description: A vast canyon system running along the Martian equator, stretching over 4,000 km long and up to 7 km deep.

name: Jupiter
description: Jupiter is more than twice as massive than the other planets of our solar system combined. The giant planet's Great Red Spot is a centuries-old storm bigger than Earth, swirling in a dense atmosphere of hydrogen and helium.

hotspots.name: Great Red Spot
hotspots.description: An enormous, spinning storm in Jupiter's atmosphere that is twice the width of Earth and has raged for at least 300 years.

name: Saturn
description: Adorned with a dazzling, complex system of icy rings, Saturn is unique in our solar system. The other giant planets have rings, but none are as spectacular or as complicated as Saturn's. It is a gas giant made mostly of hydrogen and helium.

hotspots.name: Hexagonal North Pole
hotspots.description: A bizarre, six-sided jet stream storm at Saturn's north pole, spanning wider than two Earths.

name: Uranus
description: Uranus is an ice giant that orbits the Sun on its side, tilted at an extreme angle of 97.7 degrees. This unique tilt causes extreme seasons and makes Uranus rotate almost like a rolling ball.

hotspots.name: Extreme Axial Tilt
hotspots.description: Uranus's spin axis is tilted by 98 degrees, likely due to a collision with an Earth-sized body in its early history.

name: Neptune
description: Neptune, the most distant major planet orbiting our Sun, is a dark, cold, ice giant whipped by supersonic winds. It was the first planet located through mathematical calculations rather than direct observation.

hotspots.name: Great Dark Spot
hotspots.description: A series of massive, dark storm systems in Neptune's atmosphere, characterized by winds reaching up to 2,100 km/h.

name: Pluto
description: Pluto, once considered the ninth planet, is the most famous dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt. It is a complex, icy world with glaciers of nitrogen, blue skies, and towering mountains of water ice.

hotspots.name: Tombaugh Regio
hotspots.description: A vast, bright, heart-shaped region composed of nitrogen, carbon monoxide, and methane snows, nicknamed Pluto's Heart.
